title: jasione montana subsp. glabra
intro: Jasione montana subsp. glabra, also known as hairy bluebells, is a subspecies of the Jasione montana plant, a type of flowering plant in the bellflower family native to the mountains of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Jasione montana subsp. glabra include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
